mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, discount rates on equities decline and risk appetite improves, so the S&P 500 tends to rise over the next month. This signal is strongest when the rate path is clearly easing rather than flat.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a short-term threshold signals earnings risk, slower consumption, and tighter financial conditions, which should pressure the S&P 500 lower over the next month. The effect requires an actual unemployment break, not just noise.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day returns more than 50% of the time, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening household income and retail demand, which should support equity prices over the next quarter. The signal is interaction-driven because labor gains matter more when sales are also expanding.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and rising RSAFS does not outperform either series alone by at least 20% in hit rate for 60-day forward returns, or if the combined signal's Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO after a prior contraction marks stabilizing industrial output and lower recession probability, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is only active when the economy is emerging from a production slump.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases for 2 consecutive months after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if recovery triggers are followed by negative 20-day returns more than 45% of the time, or if the mean return after trigger events is not greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL remains high while FEDFUNDS stays very low, real policy rates are deeply negative and liquidity conditions are easy, which should lift the S&P 500 over the following month. The mechanism fails if inflation eases faster than policy stays loose.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation stays above 3% year over year while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%
regime_hint: inflationary easing with low policy rates
failure_condition: Reject if the high-inflation/low-rate regime does not outperform the unconditional 20-day return distribution, or if positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST signals weaker residential construction and spillovers into demand, employment, and credit, which should reduce S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect is stronger when housing is rolling over from an already elevated level.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of housing-downturn triggers, or if the average forward return is not materially below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth together with low unemployment indicates a still-healthy labor market that supports earnings and consumption, so the S&P 500 should gain over the next month. This is an interaction because payroll gains are more informative when unemployment is not rising.
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS growth combined with flat or falling UNRATE fails to beat the unconditional hit rate by at least 10 percentage points, or if the rolling 2-year IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in INDPRO combined with rising UNRATE identifies a recessionary supply-demand breakdown that should pressure equities lower over the next week. The signal is short-horizon because markets react quickly to confirmed cyclical deterioration.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by at least 0.5% over 2 months and UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over the same span
failure_condition: Reject if triggered recession-scare episodes do not produce negative 5-day forward returns more often than positive ones, or if the average 5-day return after triggers is not below zero.
